# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Docker Compose file for ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) and APM Server. Configure all services with network settings. Set up communication between components.
# Category: monitoring_misconfiguration

version: '3' # Version specification to conform to the new version of docker-compose 
services:    # Services section  
  elasticsearch:      # Name for each service (similar as ID in an standalone Docker)             
     image: "docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch"      
     environment:        # Container variables            
         - node.name=es01         
         - cluster.initial_master_nodes=es01, es02     
         - cluster.initial_node_data_paths=es01:/var/lib/elasticsearch/, es02:/var/lib/elasticsearch      
     ports:                  # Expose container port    (default is random)                      
          - 9300:9300     
        #- "4567:-//localhost"   Use this if you want to expose Logstash on a different port            
     networks:                # Declare the services as network connections        
       elk_network:           # Name of our custom service                  (similar with id)             
          driver: bridge      # Type is set by default, use other drivers like overlay if needed. 
        logstash-service:   # Service that will connect to Elasticsearch and Kibana      
          links:                # Define connections        
            - elasticsearch   
             es01              # Alias for IP of the host (default) or name in another docker service      elk_network  logsstash-service=es02        apmserver           Elastic APM Server   kibana       KIBANA SERVER INFORMATION: https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/apm/agent/kibana/5.x/setup-kibana.html
